text:
In the heart of a quiet town on the edge of summer, an unlikely adventure began. The sun was a warm ball climbing high into the sky, casting long shadows upon the cobblestoned walks, and a gentle breeze carried sweet cicada songs from the deep woodlands.

It was Judy who first spotted it. In the midst of her afternoon chores, she leaned on the fence by the vegetable patch, eyes tracing the path the sun took across the sky. A small, glittering object fell out of nowhere—a mirror. It reflected none of the beauty around it, but something magical.

She stuck it in her pocket. That evening, in the corner of her cozy kitchen, Judy sat staring at her reflection. "You know, darling," she murmured, "you might just be the most extraordinary thing we've ever found."

The next morning, she carried the mirror to the forest. The dew glistened on the grass as she stepped into the clearing. A deer antler caught
